gauge.name: Fox River at Montgomery

impacts.statement: Batavia City Hall and Batavia Police Department threatened.

Structures threatened at Stolp Island in downtown Aurora. Structures threatened at Cool Acres Park in Aurora.  River Street near Sullivan Road threatened in Aurora. IL-25 threatened near Laurel Street in Batavia.
Industrial structures are threatened along Harrison Street in Oswego.

A few residences threatened along IL-25 (East River Road) in Montgomery. A few residences are threatened along Marina Drive between Montgomery and Oswego. Low-lying parking lots threatened in downtown Aurora.

Mooseheart wastewater treatment plant property is threatened. A few structures threatened along River Street near South River Street Park in Aurora. River Street is threatened at South River Street Park and near Ashland Avenue in Aurora.
Batavia wastewater treatment plant property is threatened.
Fox Metro Wastewater Reclamation facility property is threatened south of Montomgery.
IL-25 (East River Road) is threatened near 1st Avenue in Montgomery. Harrison Street is threatened in Oswego. Flinn Street and Shumway Avenue threatened in Batavia.

A couple structures threatened in downtown Batavia. IL-25 (East River Road) is threatened near Boulder Hill Pass. Numerous sections of Fox River Trail are threatened in Oswego. Low-lying trails threatened at Lippold Park between Batavia and North Aurora. Athletic fields at South River Street Park are inundated in Aurora.

Residences are threatened along River Street near Ashland Avenue in Montgomery. Veterans Memorial threatened at Illinois Avenue in Aurora. Low-lying sections of Hudson Crossing Park are inundated in Oswego.
Athletic fields threatened at Clark Island Park in Batavia.

Low-lying park land inundated along IL-25 between Montomgery and Oswego. Low-lying sections of Fabyan Forest Preserve near Batavia, Hurds Island and Gregory Island Park in Aurora, and Riverfront Park in North Aurora are inundated.
Low-lying parking lots at city hall and Boy Scout Island are threatened in St. Charles. Fox River Trail is threatened at St. Mary Park in St. Charles.
Water overtops low-lying banks near Montgomery, St. Charles, and Clark Island Park in Batavia.

impactsLowWaters.impact: Water supply intakes at Aurora Water Treatment Plant may be threatened. Water supply for the city of Aurora can be supplemented by groundwater sources.
River reaches crest elevation of Montgomery Dam. River nears zero flow.
Approximate stage of the 1-in-10 annual chance flow (7-day average).

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past and future precipitation.  The  stages/flows that will occur may be different if future rainfall is different than forecast.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
